chapter_goal: Teach surface geometry through invariant ledgers: intersection forms, blowups, rulings, birational graphs, classification geography, and Noether corrections.

visual_sequence.concept: Intersection numbers, adjunction, and Riemann-Roch on surfaces
visual_sequence.inspection_target: self-intersection and canonical pairing determine genus through adjunction
each blowup adds a -1 exceptional class and redirects a rational map through a resolution
visual_sequence.invariant: intersection form gains negative exceptional diagonal entries
visual_sequence.representation: exceptional-curve graph and transformed intersection form

visual_sequence.concept: Quadric surface rulings and cubic-surface incidence
visual_sequence.inspection_target: two curve families sweep the quadric surface
visual_sequence.invariant: sampled ruling curves lie on x*y-z*w=0 in affine coordinates
visual_sequence.representation: interactive ruled surface plus incidence graph

visual_sequence.concept: Classification geography, Noether formula, and singularity corrections
visual_sequence.inspection_target: surface types occupy different invariant positions and correction terms alter Noether counts
visual_sequence.invariant: Noether ledger balances for sampled smooth and corrected cases
visual_sequence.representation: surface invariant scatter and correction table
